title: Add crisis support popup with keyword detection for suicidal ideation

requirements.summary: Create a CrisisSupportModal component that displays a prominent, full-screen-overlay popup with a compassionate message, free 30-minute session offer, contact number '90xoxo90xo', and freely dismissible close button
acceptanceCriteria: Modal renders as a prominent overlay with high visual contrast (e.g., warm, supportive color scheme) that draws immediate attention
Modal displays a compassionate headline such as 'You Are Not Alone' and a supportive body message
Modal displays an offer for a free 30-minute session with the contact number '90xoxo90xo' clearly visible and easy to read/copy
Modal includes a visible close/dismiss button that closes it without any forced action
Modal is accessible with proper ARIA roles (role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby)

file_operations.description: Create a new CrisisSupportModal component that renders a full-screen overlay dialog with warm, supportive styling. Display the headline 'You Are Not Alone', a compassionate message body, an offer for a free 30-minute session with a real therapist, and the contact number '90xoxo90xo' prominently. Include a clearly visible close button that dismisses the modal without requiring acknowledgement. Implement proper ARIA attributes (role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby) for accessibility. Use glass-morphism styling consistent with the MindVault design system. Accept props for controlling visibility (isOpen, onClose).

requirements.summary: Implement keyword detection logic in the frontend that scans user-submitted text for crisis-related phrases indicating suicidal ideation and triggers the CrisisSupportModal when detected
acceptanceCriteria: A utility function (e.g., detectCrisisKeywords) exists that takes a string and returns true if any crisis phrase is matched (case-insensitive)
AnonymousChat.tsx calls this detection function on each message submission and shows CrisisSupportModal when true
DataEntryPanel.tsx calls this detection function on the submitted text values and shows CrisisSupportModal when true
Detection is case-insensitive and matches partial phrases within a longer sentence
Crisis modal appears immediately after submission if keywords are detected, before or alongside any other success feedback

file_operations.description: Create a utility module that exports a detectCrisisKeywords function. The function accepts a string and returns true if any crisis-related phrases are detected (case-insensitive): 'suicide', 'suicidal', 'end my life', 'kill myself', 'don't want to be here', 'want to die', 'no reason to live', 'better off dead', 'can't go on', 'take my own life'. Implement case-insensitive matching that works with partial phrases within longer sentences. Use string normalization (toLowerCase) and substring or regex matching for robust detection.

Import detectCrisisKeywords from lib/crisisDetection and CrisisSupportModal component. Add state to manage modal visibility (e.g., useState for showCrisisModal). In the message submission handler, call detectCrisisKeywords on the user's message text before or after posting. If true is returned, set showCrisisModal to true to display the modal immediately. Render the CrisisSupportModal component at the end of the component JSX with isOpen={showCrisisModal} and onClose handler that sets showCrisisModal to false. Ensure the modal appears before or alongside any success toast/notification.

Import detectCrisisKeywords from lib/crisisDetection and CrisisSupportModal component. Add state to manage modal visibility (e.g., useState for showCrisisModal). In the data entry submission handler, call detectCrisisKeywords on the submitted value field (the text content). If true is returned, set showCrisisModal to true to display the modal immediately. Render the CrisisSupportModal component at the end of the component JSX with isOpen={showCrisisModal} and onClose handler that sets showCrisisModal to false. Ensure the modal appears before or alongside any success toast/notification.
